item.title: Sukundimi Walks Before Me nominated for awards
item.description: [Act Now PNG] The striking documentary 'Sukundimi Walks Before Me' has been nominated for both the Documentary Australia Award for Best Documentary and the Sustainable Future Award

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts: Independence statement from the President of Bougainville
item.description: [Bougainville News] My fellow Bougainvilleans, In the next few days, the resolve of the 97.8 percent of Bougainvilleans who voted for Bougainville to attain independence will be tested by the tabling of the Bi-Partisan Report on the 2019 Bougainville Referendum in the … Continue reading →

item.title: Exclusive: Fiji’s Reserve Bank Governor on what will drive the country’s next phase of growth
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] In an exclusive video interview for the forthcoming Business Advantage Fiji annual, Reserve Bank of Fiji Governor Ariff Ali considers which sectors will drive Fiji's growth in coming years, and what investments will be required to support them. The post Exclusive: Fiji’s Reserve Bank Governor on wha

item.title: Request for Expression of Interest – Monitoring and Evaluation Firm (CS02)
item.description: [Education News PNG] The Department of Higher Education, Research Science and Technology (DHERST) through the Improved for TVET Employment Project is requesting Expressions of Interest for the following The post Request for Expression of Interest – Monitoring and Evaluation Firm (CS02) first appeared on Education News P

item.title: Experts Urge Preparedness As El Niño Threatens Food And Water Security
item.description: [Inside PNG] With the onset of El Niño, Agricultural experts are warning that the country faces a high probability of a shortened wet season, likely followed by extended periods of below-average rainfall. National Agricultural Research Institute is encouraging the public and farmers to look at options to save cl

item.title: Maroons Stage Second-Half Comeback to Level Origin Series
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Queensland has forced the 2026 State of Origin series into a deciding third match after producing a devastating second-half display to defeat New South Wales 44-24 before a record crowd at Melbourne's iconic cricket ground on Wednesday night. Trailing 12-8 at the interval, the Maroons transformed th

item.title: Youth Unemployment in PNG
item.description: [Pawa TV] Youth unemployment in Papua New Guinea poses significant socio-economic challenges, with many school leavers struggling to find formal employment. Despite government initiatives, structural barriers persist, risking long-term underemployment and poverty. The post Youth Unemployment in PNG first appe

item.title: The Coolest Vanity Apps for You and Your Girls
item.description: [PNG Buzz] What’s possible in a week? If you dedicated seven days to the achievement of one goal, how ambitious could you make this goal? These were the questions that the multilingual friends Katy and Sara posed themselves when they determined to learn English in one week, to prove that it can be done and any

item.title: Queensland Maroons Stage Stunning Second-Half Revival to Force Origin Decider
item.description: [PNG Facts] In front of a record-breaking crowd of 91,671 at the Melbourne Cricket Ground, the Maroons produced a devastating second-half performance to overpower New South Wales 44-24, ensuring the 2026 State of Origin series will be settled at Brisbane's Suncorp Stadium. The capacity attendance surpassed the

item.title: Toroama says Parliament decision breaches Melanesian Agreement
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] Bougainville President has described the National Parliament’s decision to adopt a draft sessional order on the results of the Bougainville Referendum as a breach of the Melanesian Agreement and a move that undermines the Bougainville peace process. In a media statement, President Ishmael Toroama sa

item.title: ICAC to seek judicial review following District Court ruling in Don Polye case
item.description: [The PNG Sun] The Independent Commission Against Corruption (ICAC) says it is considering further legal action following a District Court ruling that dismissed criminal charges against former Kandep MP Don Polye. In a statement released yesterday, ICAC confirmed it had received the ruling delivered by Magistrate

item.title: Pisin mekim na balus bungim hevi long Goroka
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Ol pisin i bin pasim ranwe long Goroka ples balus na i givim hevi long wanpela balus bilong PNG Air. Tupela wil bilong balus i bruk tasol no gat manmeri i kisim bagarap long dispela hevi.

item.title: Illegal logging will sink government's timber processing plans
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Government plan to establish four regional centres for timber processing will fail unless it first puts an end to widespread illegal logging

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts :Bougainville Landowners demand mining companies halt reopening of copper pit
item.description: [Bougainville News] Traditional landowners are fighting back against plans to reopen an existing Bougainville copper mine which has remained dormant for nearly four decades. During a public event on Bougainville Island last week a warning was declared against the companies who landowners … Continue reading →

item.title: Expansion on the cards for one of Papua New Guinea’s leading corporate healthcare providers
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Medical services provider Aspen Medical PNG has grown significantly in recent years, mainly thanks to demand from one industry sector in particular. Senior Medical Officer Dr Moses Lester outlines its growth strategy to Business Advantage PNG . The post Expansion on the cards for one of Papua New Gu

item.title: Fight at Paglum Adventist Secondary School Leaves 16 Students Injured, Classes Suspended
item.description: [Education News PNG] Western Highlands, Papua New Guinea – Classes at Paglum Adventist Secondary School in Western Highlands Province have been suspended for one week following a violent The post Fight at Paglum Adventist Secondary School Leaves 16 Students Injured, Classes Suspended first appeared on Education News PNG

item.title: How Solar Tools and Family Farming Revived Sepik’s Vanilla
item.description: [Inside PNG] For nine months, a vanilla farmer waits. That is the time between the orchid flower and the mature bean, requiring a long period of delicate manual labor that begins with hand-pollinating every single bloom. For years, farmers in East Sepik Province were trapped in a destructive cycle that ruined th

item.title: PNG Court Clears Don Polye of Corruption Charges
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Former Kandep MP Don Polye has been cleared of three serious criminal allegations after the Waigani District Court dismissed the case against him on Tuesday. The ruling brings an end to a high-profile legal battle that had placed the veteran politician under scrutiny over allegations linked to state

item.title: Proposed Polygamy Law in PNG
item.description: [Pawa TV] Polygamy in Papua New Guinea is rooted in tradition, but the government, recognising its impact on gender equality, plans to introduce laws banning it. Consultations will precede this reform. The post Proposed Polygamy Law in PNG first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.title: PNG Works Department Moves to Digital Payments System
item.description: [PNG Facts] The Department of Works and Highways is shifting away from paper-based payments as PNG's public sector continues its move towards digital financial management. The introduction of Electronic Funds Transfer (EFT) within the department is expected to improve the speed of payments, strengthen accountab

item.title: Frank reappointed as NCDC City Manager for another 4 years
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By GEORGINA MICHAEL RAVU Frank has been reappointed as National Capital District City Manager for a second term following the expiry of his four-year contract on 8th of June 2026. NCDC Governor Powes Parkop announced the reappointment at a press conference, saying the decision was made unanimously b

item.title: Solomon Airlines granted International License to operate Port Moresby-Honiara Route
item.description: [The PNG Sun] The Department of Transport (DOT) has granted Solomon Airlines Limited an International Airline License to operate scheduled international air services between Honiara, Solomon Islands, and Port Moresby, Papua New Guinea, following approval under the Civil Aviation Act 2000 (as amended). The approva

item.title: Illegal logging will sink government’s downstream processing plans
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Government’s ambitious plan to establish four regional centres for timber processing and value-adding will fail unless it first puts an end to widespread illegal logging

item.title: Bougainville News Alert : Statement from the Office of the President: Response to Chief John L Momis, GCL, former President of Bougainville
item.description: [Bougainville News] It is disappointing to see a leader of John Momis’ stature, a former two term President of Bougainville and longtime Regional Member for Bougainville trying to undermine the good work of the Autonomous Bougainville Government. See Momis Press Release Part … Continue reading →

item.title: ‘We’re challenging ourselves and our competitors to do better’: Kina Bank’s CEO on the push for innovation in PNG’s finance sector
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Kina Bank has been leading the way in bringing digital banking products to Papua New Guineans, most recently with the launch of the Pei Beta digital wallet earlier this year. Its CEO Ivan Vidovich shared his bank’s vision on digital payments and innovation in an on-stage interview at the Innovation

item.title: Water PNG: Emergency Repairs to Cause Port Moresby Water Shortages
item.description: [Inside PNG] Port Moresby’s daily water supply could fall from 180million litres during emergency repairs to the Rouna 1/3 raw water pipeline, Water PNG has confirmed. Chief Executive officer James Young says this is not a total shutdown of the city’s water supply, despite claims circulating on social media. “Th

item.title: PNG Adopts Faster Payment Processes as Works Department Goes Digital
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] PNG's drive towards digital government services has reached another milestone, with the Department of Works and Highways preparing to replace manual payment methods with electronic transactions. The move is expected to improve the management of public funds, reduce operating costs and provide cleare

item.title: The Origins of Tok Pisin
item.description: [Pawa TV] Tok Pisin is one of the main languages spoken in Papua New Guinea (PNG) and plays a vital role in communication across the country. With over 800 languages spoken nationwide, Tok Pisin serves as a unifying language that allows people from different regions to understand each other. It is an English-

item.title: Divine Word University Opens New Dormitory to Support PNG's Future Doctors
item.description: [PNG Facts] Medical students training at ANGAU Memorial Hospital will now have access to dedicated accommodation following the opening of a new dormitory developed by Divine Word University in Lae. University leaders say the facility will strengthen clinical training opportunities, support increased enrolment i

item.title: Ear Disease in Morobe
item.description: [Post Courier] Rural health teams in Morobe are warning that ear disease is still being found too late, with many patients already suffering permanent damage. item.title: PM & NEC Dept moves to electronic payments, ends cheque system for service provider payments
item.description: [The PNG Sun] The Department of Prime Minister and National Executive Council (DPMNEC) has transitioned from cheque payments to an Electronic Funds Transfer (EFT) system, marking a major step towards modernizing government financial operations. Finance Secretary Samuel Penias and Chief Secretary to Government Iva

item.title: Graun bruk katim Sepik Haiwe
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Bikpela ren i mekim graun i bruk na bagarapim rot long Sepik Haiwe. Dispela rot i save bungim Is na Wes Sepik na i go olgeta long boda bilong PNG wantaim Indonesia.

item.title: PNG Correctional Officers Held After Firearms Incident in Port Moresby
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] About 20 Papua New Guinea Correctional Service officers are being held by police following an incident involving the alleged illegal discharge of firearms and threats made against members of the public in Port Moresby. Police allege the disturbance was linked to reports surrounding the unofficial ap

item.title: The Green Fee / Environment Levy in PNG
item.description: [Pawa TV] Papua New Guinea has introduced a K50 Green Fee for departing international travellers to fund environmental protection and climate resilience efforts, amidst significant challenges posed by climate change and biodiversity loss. item.title: PNG Economy Gets Major Boost from Port Moresby Growth
item.description: [PNG Facts] Rapid urban expansion and billions of kina worth of investment projects are helping Port Moresby account for around 30 per cent of Papua New Guinea’s gross domestic product, according to NCD Governor Powes Parkop. The Governor said the nation's capital continues to attract substantial private and pu

item.title: EFT implementation to reduce costs for the dept: Secretary Holemba
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By GINNAH MINI Secretary for the Department of Works and Highways (DoWH), Gibson Holemba, says the new Electronic Funds Transfer (EFT) system will reduce costs—specifically for Provincial Works Managers who previously had to travel to Port Moresby to collect cheques. item.title: Gerehu Secondary School receives over 1’000 books to boost student literacy
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Gerehu Secondary School in Port Moresby has received more than 1,000 new books to strengthen its library resources and support learning for students from Grades 7 to 12. The donation was delivered through a partnership between the University of Papua New Guinea Hagen Urban Students Association and l

item.title: Gavman Minista bam long kar long haiwe
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Minista bilong 'Energy' na Memba bilong Kairuku, Peter Namea Isoaimo i bin bam long kar long 2nd Februeri, 2026 long Hiritano Haiwe. Em i bam long kar wantaim wanpela soldia bilong PNG Defence Force (PNGDF). Ol kar bilong tupela man i stap nau long polis stesin long 15 Mile na wok painimaut i gohet

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts : Simon Pentanu : My memories of Kieta
item.description: [Bougainville News] The first permanent German presence in Kieta, Bougainville, began with the establishment of a Marist mission in 1901, followed by an official German colonial administrative office in 1905 to oversee the region. They would have seen, steaming into the deep natural harbour, … Continue reading →

item.title: High tech and low tech: Innovation PNG 2026 Awards winners announced
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] The winners of Papua New Guinea’s major awards for innovation were announced at a special breakfast on 3 June at the Hilton Hotel in Port Moresby. While two of the awards were built on PNG's growing digital backbone, the third award involved a decidedly offline solution. The post High tech and low t

item.title: Community Volunteers Join PNG Police Crime Prevention Drive at Nine Mile Port Moresby
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Forty-five community volunteers have joined a new policing initiative in PNG's Nine Mile and Bomana areas aimed at strengthening cooperation between residents and law enforcement agencies. The volunteers were introduced during the launch of a Community Policing program at Nine Mile Police Station, a

item.title: Music Royalties and Payments for PNG Artists
item.description: [Pawa TV] Musicians in Papua New Guinea earn through royalties, but lack a domestic collective management organisation. Many rely on overseas bodies like APRA for payments, facing challenges in enforcement and broadcaster compliance. The post Music Royalties and Payments for PNG Artists first appeared on PAWA

item.description: [PNG Facts] Port Moresby’s waterfront is set for another major transformation with the commencement of the Kumul Haus development, a project National Capital District Governor Powes Parkop says will strengthen the city’s appeal to investors and reshape a key section of Papua New Guinea’s capital. item.description: [Post Courier] A new residence hall named after Dame Jean Kekedo has opened at the Papua New Guinea University of Technology in Lae, aimed at helping more women access higher education. item.title: Prime Minister Marape announces K5 million immediate relief support for Manus volcanic pumice disaster
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] PRIME Minister James Marape has announced an immediate K5 million National Government relief support package for Manus Province following the unusual undersea volcanic activity in the Bismarck Sea that has caused large deposits of volcanic pumice and other marine discharge to wash onto the shores of

item.title: Port Moresby City Manager Ravu Frank re-appointed for second term in office
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Port Moresby City Manager Ravu Frank has been reappointed for a second term in office. The term of his first appointment lapsed this month. The duration of his reappointment is for another 4 years. Related news: https://www.postcourier.com.pg/ncdc-city-manager-reappointed/ He signed his contract ins

item.title: PNG Government Targets Online Abuse Through Sevis Digital ID System

item.title: Electric Cars in PNG

As part of the ICC campaign, Cricket PNG joins more tha

item.title: No more markets at three main Waigani bus stops: Police
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Waigani Station Commander, Max Makeso has announced that no markets will be allowed to operate at the three main bus-stops in Waigani, Port Moresby as police intensify efforts to maintain law and order and improve public safety. The affected locations are the Waigani-Morata, Waigani-Tokarara, and Wa

The National Airports Corporation has launched a large-scale rehabilitation program that will enable the airpor

item.title: Corporal and Capital Punishment in Papua New Guinea
item.description: [Pawa TV] Papua New Guinea has abolished capital punishment, replacing it with life imprisonment, while corporal punishment remains legally permitted in some contexts, highlighting ongoing tensions between tradition, law reform and human rights advocacy. The post Corporal and Capital Punishment in Papua New G

The expo, which opened at the Hilton Hotel yesterday, is being held alongside the party's nation

item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] ACTING Minister for Education Kinoka Feo has reaffirmed the government’s priority of providing safe, secure, and strictly educational internet access for students and teachers as Papua New Guinea rolls out the new “EDUNEX” National Digital Learning Management System. The Department of Education is d

item.title: PNG Athlete finishes fourth at World Yogasana Championship in India
item.description: [The PNG Sun] A male Papua New Guinean athlete from a Port Moresby settlement has proudly represented the country on the world stage. He finished fourth at the 2026 World Yogasana Championship held in Ahmedabad, India this month. Related news: https://www.olympics.com/en/news/world-yogasana-championship-2026-repo

item.title: Australia pasim tingting bilong PNG wantaim ragbi lig
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Australia i bilip ol i pasim tingting na bel bilong Papaua Niugini pinis taim ol i sapotim PNG long putim wanpela tim insait long NRL. Ol i bilip dispela i pasim PNG na i no wari long ol arapela wokbung PNG i gat wantaim ol arapela kantri olsem China.

item.title: Call on Forest Minister and Board to explain failure to cancel FCA permits
item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW! is calls on the Forest Minster and National Forest Board to explain why controversial FCA logging permits have not been cancelled.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts :Rio Tinto urged to accelerate action on remediation of Panguna mine disaster, one year on from investigation
item.description: [Bougainville News] One year on from the release of an independent investigation into Rio Tinto’s former Panguna mine in Bougainville, communities living with the ongoing environmental impact are calling on the company to urgently move towards funding solutions, particularly in areas identified … Continue reading →

item.title: Meet some of Papua New Guinea’s most innovative large businesses
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] A record number of corporations entered the 2026 Innovation PNG Awards this year, with the shortlists announced earlier this month in Port Moresby. From a digital platform for strata titles to a system to streamline coffee exports, we consider the five projects shortlisted for this year’s corporate

item.title: USP Pro-Chancellor challenges new generation of Pacific leadersat Emalus graduation
item.description: [Education News PNG] As The University of the South Pacific (USP) graduation season continues across the region, the Emalus Campus ceremony in Vanuatu today served as a powerful The post USP Pro-Chancellor challenges new generation of Pacific leadersat Emalus graduation first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: Valentine Holmes Declares for Cook Islands Ahead of World Cup
item.description: [Inside PNG] NRL star Valentine Holmes commits to the Cook Islands for the Rugby League World Cup to honor his mother’s heritage and mentor the Aitu squad. The post Valentine Holmes Declares for Cook Islands Ahead of World Cup first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Valentine Holmes Declares for Cook Islands Ahe

item.title: PNG Eyes Economic Growth Through Major LNG and Mining Projects
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Papua New Guinea is preparing for what the Government describes as a long period of economic expansion as major resource projects move closer to development. Prime Minister James Marape says the Papua LNG project in Gulf Province and the Wafi-Golpu project in Morobe Province are among several large

item.title: The Police Tattoo
item.description: [Pawa TV] The Police Tattoo in Papua New Guinea celebrates policing tradition and cultural pride through band performances, strengthening regional cooperation and community trust, while marking a significant milestone in PNG's history. The post The Police Tattoo first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.title: Different New Year Celebrations Around the World
item.description: [PNG Buzz] What’s possible in a week? If you dedicated seven days to the achievement of one goal, how ambitious could you make this goal? These were the questions that the multilingual friends Katy and Sara posed themselves when they determined to learn English in one week, to prove that it can be done and any

item.title: UPNG Eyes AI Future While Emphasizing Ethics and Human Control
item.description: [PNG Facts] Artificial intelligence is reshaping the modern world at an unprecedented pace, and the University of Papua New Guinea believes education must evolve just as quickly to meet the challenge. UPNG Vice Chancellor Professor Ian Findlay says the rise of AI presents enormous opportunities but also demands

item.title: PNG pleased Hormuz Strait will reopen
item.description: [Post Courier] Papua New Guinea’s Foreign Affairs Minister Justin Tkatchenko has welcomed the agreement between the United States and Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, describing it as vital for regional stability and energy security. To access this post, you must purchase Web , Web – One-Day , Web – 14-Day , W

item.title: 23 CLRC Officers Inducted into the Public Service
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] TWENTY-THREE officers from the Constitutional and Law Reform Commission (CLRC) have successfully completed the week-long Induction Training for Public Servants and were formally inducted into the National Public Service. The training, facilitated by Mr. Robin Pala, covered key aspects of the public

item.title: Cocoa Board upskills officers to strengthen PNG Cocoa Industry
item.description: [The PNG Sun] The Cocoa Board of Papua New Guinea (CBPNG) has launched a nationwide training program aimed at strengthening extension services and improving support for cocoa farmers across the country. The two-week Training of Trainers (TOT) workshop, underway at the Tavilo Cocoa Board Research Station in East N

item.title: La Nina bai kam - gavman mas redi
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] National Weather Service (NWS) bilong PNG i tok 'La Nina' bai kam klostu nau tasol i no gat wanpela toksave i kam long gavman yet long wanem samting ol bai mekim o sapos ol i redi long wanem kain ol hevi dispela taim nogut bai nap long bringim.

item.title: Call on Forest Minister and Board to explain failure to cancel FCA permits
item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW is calling on the Forest Minster and National Forest Board to explain why controversial FCA logging permits have not been cancelled

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts : OBEC returns 45 writs, marks completion of 2025 ABG General Election
item.description: [Bougainville News] Bougainville Electoral Commissioner, Desmond Tsianai, today formally returned 45 writs comprising the Presidency, 38 single-member constituencies and the six reserved regional seats for women and Former Combatants. Tsianai formally handed the election writs to the outgoing Speaker, Simon Pentanu, of

item.title: Review: Summit Bar, Hilton Port Moresby
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Robert Hamilton-Jones returns to the rooftop Summit Bar in the Hilton Port Moresby to check out their cocktail range. The post Review: Summit Bar, Hilton Port Moresby appeared first on Business Advantage PNG .

item.title: USP Concludes Laucala Graduation with a strong focus on Pacific Leadership and Legacies
item.description: [Education News PNG] The University of the South Pacific (USP) concluded its two-day graduation ceremonies at the Laucala Campus in Suva, Fiji, today, marking another important milestone as The post USP Concludes Laucala Graduation with a strong focus on Pacific Leadership and Legacies first appeared on Education News P

item.title: Morobe’s New Tourism Authority Board
item.description: [Inside PNG] Morobe Governor and Provincial Executive Council (PEC) Chairman, Rainbo Paita, has announced the appointment of a new Morobe Tourism Authority Board following a decision of the Provincial Executive Council during its Sam Sewe Meeting No. 04/2026 held on 23–24 April 2026. The decision formally revoke

item.title: PNG Needs More Hospitals as PMGH Struggles With Rising Patient Numbers
item.description: [PNG Facts] Calls have been made for major health infrastructure expansion in the nation's capital after concerns emerged that Port Moresby General Hospital is being overwhelmed by patients who could be treated elsewhere. Speaking in Parliament, Abau MP Sir Puka Temu said the growing population of Port Moresby

item.title: Laiagam road to be used as alternate route for the Pogera gold mine
item.description: [Post Courier] The Wabag - Laiagam road network constructed by the Wabag District Development Authority starting near Sopas District Hospital in Wabag District and joining Yokonda in the Pilikambi LLG of the new Lagaip District will be a shorter, safe and convenient route into the Pogera gold mine once completed.

item.title: DoWH Launches Electronic Funds Transfer System
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By GINNAH MINI THE Department of Works and Highways (DoWH) has launched its Electronic Funds Transfer (EFT) payment system today-replacing the manual cheque payments to improve efficiency, transparency, accountability and service delivery in the department. The Department of Finance has implemented

item.title: PM Marape re-endorsed as Pangu Leader ahead of 2027 Election
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Prime Minister and Pangu Pati Leader, James Marape has been unanimously re-endorsed to lead the party into the 2027 National General Election following the conclusion of Pangu Pati’s 28th National Convention in Port Moresby. Mr. Marape said delegates endorsed his leadership after he temporarily step

item.title: K8 billion lost in illicit forestry activities under investigation
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Estimated K8.78 billion lost through illicit forestry activities and suspected money laundering schemes is now at the centre of four major investigations under Operation Arcadia, a multi-agency crackdown supported by INTERPOL.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts : Could tourism lead Bougainville into the future ?
item.description: [Bougainville News] Bougainville has gone through many turbulent periods over the past 100 years, not least the Civil War of the 1990s, which claimed as many as 20,000 lives. The government is now looking to develop the economy and has its eye … Continue reading →

item.title: The other LEO satellite provider: Papua New Guinea business begin trials of OneWeb
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] While Starlink was approved for use in PNG in late April to much fanfare, the approval of another LEO service – OneWeb – has received relatively little attention. Robbie Huxley, Managing Director of TE PNG, tells Business Advantage PNG why OneWeb’s arrival is such an important moment for Papua New G

item.title: USP honours more than 60 high achievers at Medals and Prizes Ceremony
item.description: [Education News PNG] More than 60 outstanding students and graduates of The University of the South Pacific (USP) were recognised for academic excellence at the Medals and Prizes The post USP honours more than 60 high achievers at Medals and Prizes Ceremony first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: Brotherly love meets rivalry in M1 derby
item.description: [Inside PNG] Brothers Payne and Klese Haas clash in the M1 derby as Brisbane hosts Gold Coast. "He gives me a paragraph of stuff to work on," Klese says. The post Brotherly love meets rivalry in M1 derby first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Brotherly love meets rivalry in M1 derby appeared first on Inside PNG

item.title: Student among Three Killed Following Enga Tribal Compensation Dispute
item.description: [PNG Facts] A young schoolboy and two men have lost their lives after a compensation ceremony descended into violence in Enga Province, raising fears of further tribal unrest in the area. The deaths occurred in Lagaip District where clans had gathered to settle differences through a BEL KOL payment ceremony, bu

item.title: Bougainville reflects on journey of resilience and self-determination
item.description: [Post Courier] Speaker of the Bougainville House of Representatives Simon Pentanu has called on Bougainvilleans to draw strength from their shared history of sacrifice as the region advances its pursuit of greater sovereignty. Addressing crowds at this year's Bougainville Foundation Day celebrations, Pentanu descr

item.title: DoWH, NHC Partner to Deliver Housing Opportunities for Public Servants
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By ESWIN PLESIO PUBLIC servants at the Department of Works and Highways (DoWH) are set to benefit from new housing opportunities following the signing of a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) between the department and the National Housing Corporation (NHC). The agreement marks the start of a partners

item.title: New Office, four vehicles boost CIR as Acting Registrar takes charge
item.description: [The PNG Sun] The Papua New Guinea Civil and Identity Registry (CIR) has entered a new phase of operations with the commissioning of a new office facility, delivery of four vehicles and the appointment of Acting Registrar-General Leslie Simon. The transition was marked at a handover ceremony at Moale Haus in Port

item.title: Government fails to answer UN concerns on illegal FCA logging and land abuses
item.description: [Act Now PNG] The Papua New Guinea government has failed to answer questions from the United Nations over the misuse of Forest Clearing Authority (FCA) logging licences and controversial land leases.

item.title: PNG Government Invests K877 Million in Student Education Support
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea's education sector has received a major boost, with more than 2.5 million students benefiting from the Government's tuition fee subsidy initiative since the Marape-Rosso Government took office. Prime Minister James Marape The scale of the support was outlined by Prime Minister James

item.title: Government fails to answer UN concerns on illegal FCA logging and land abuses
item.description: [Act Now PNG] The Papua New Guinea government has failed to answer questions from the United Nations over the misuse of Forest Clearing Authority (FCA) logging licences and controversial SABL land leases.

item.title: PNG Digital ID to Help Track Fake Facebook Users
item.description: [PNG Facts] Efforts to curb fake Facebook accounts and anonymous online attacks in Papua New Guinea are set to receive a boost through the Government’s new Sevis PNG Digital ID platform. Acting ICT Minister Peter Tsiamalili Jr says the system will help law enforcement agencies and regulators verify the identiti
